source.title: Hippocampal sharp wave-ripple: A cognitive biomarker for episodic memory and planning

confidence_rationale: Canonical, high-quality review with direct evidence for SWR during quiescent offline states, CA3 recurrent dynamics as internal generator, compressed sequence replay, and consolidation function. Directly maps onto MECH-092 (quiescent E3 heartbeat → hippocampal replay). Strong source; mapping is structurally faithful.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Sharp-wave ripples (SWRs) are generated by CA3 recurrent excitatory dynamics during offline/quiescent states (rest, slow-wave sleep, inter-trial intervals); they replay compressed (~10-20x faster) sequences of recent experience for memory consolidation; disrupting SWRs during rest impairs subsequent memory performance.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-092: During quiescent E3 heartbeat cycles (no salient incoming event), the hippocampal module performs SWR-equivalent replay — replaying compressed trajectory experience for viability map consolidation. The CA3 recurrent dynamics are the biological substrate. The quiescent-trigger matches E3 heartbeat quiescence. Consolidation function matches viability map update. V2 ARC-018 FAIL is partly explained by absence of any replay mechanism in the substrate.
mapping.mapping_caveat: SWR replay in biological hippocampus operates over seconds-to-minutes timescales during rest or sleep; the REE micro-DMN replay is designed for within-session quiescent heartbeat cycles (seconds timescale between actions). The timescale compression is an architectural decision not directly validated by this literature. CA3 recurrent dynamics are the correct substrate model, but the REE HippocampalModule does not currently implement CA3-equivalent recurrent connectivity.
mapping.source_context: Comprehensive review of rodent and human hippocampal SWR physiology; sleep, rest, and awake quiescence states; memory consolidation behavior.

confidence_components.notes: Extremely high source quality; mapping is direct for the quiescent-replay principle; main risk is timescale difference between biological SWR consolidation and REE micro-DMN replay.
